lyrics: city lights and midnight drives keep us awake forever
broken hearts and neon tears under rainy skies
dusty roads and old guitars sing about home
thunder drums and fire riffs shaking every wall
slow piano whispers in an empty room tonight
sunrise rhythm on the beach we move together
poets in the subway writing love in smoke
heavy bass and strobe lights calling out my name
church bells and southern wind carry my prayer
we scream the chorus till the speakers break
quiet rain and coffee cups by the window
golden highway dream with radio static
fractured memories on a cold november night
boots in the mud and stars above the field
laser hearts and mirrored floors in motion
my old cassette plays songs from summer
lone violin echoes in the empty station
double kick and distorted walls of sound
we found hope in a cheap motel hymn
tonight the skyline sings in silver noise
hands up we chase the dawn in crowded streets
letters never sent are buried in these lines
tractor trails and open plains in morning light
fading posters and garage amps hum alive
